full_content:
GT-KB project root boundary: Agent Red MUST live at E:\GT-KB\applications\Agent_Red\; applications/ namespace contains only deployed applications

This GOV operationalizes the project-root-boundary topology rules already present in CLAUDE.md and `.claude/rules/project-root-boundary.md`, tying them to the source DELIB (DELIB-S330-AGENT-RED-NESTED-IN-APPLICATIONS-RULE) and making them auditable as a governance specification.

FIVE BINDING RULES:

RULE 1 (root containment):
- All GT-KB project files MUST live within `E:\GT-KB`.
- No GT-KB artifact may be created, read as a live dependency, updated, verified, or required from outside that root.

RULE 2 (GT-KB-vs-application partition):
- Everything in `E:\GT-KB\` is GT-KB platform EXCEPT the contents of `E:\GT-KB\applications\`.
- Files under `E:\GT-KB\applications\<name>\` are deployed-application files belonging to the named application's project (separate identity, separate repo, separate lifecycle).

RULE 3 (Agent Red location):
- All Agent Red project files MUST live within `E:\GT-KB\applications\Agent_Red\`.
- Agent Red is a deployed application (a downstream consumer of GT-KB), not a part of GT-KB itself.
- Agent Red's canonical repo is `https://github.com/mike-remakerdigital/agent-red`.
- The `applications\Agent_Red\.git` remote MUST point to that canonical repo.

RULE 4 (applications/ namespace purity):
- The `E:\GT-KB\applications\` subdirectory MUST contain ONLY named sub-directories, each holding one deployed application.
- No other files OR non-application directories belong at `applications\` root.
- Test scaffolding directories (e.g., `applications/_test_*/` patterns) are explicitly NOT permitted at `applications\` root; they MUST live elsewhere (e.g., `tests/fixtures/`, `.tmp/`, or a dedicated test scratch directory outside `applications\`).

RULE 5 (internal organization flexibility):
- Subdirectory organization within `E:\GT-KB\` root (outside of `applications\`) is at the AI's discretion based on usability and comprehensibility.
- The AI MUST NOT flatten existing structures unless flattening is the right choice for usability/comprehensibility reasons.
- The `applications\` path itself is the only path that may NOT be altered, renamed, or restructured.

REPO TOPOLOGY (binding):
- `E:\GT-KB\.git` remote: `https://github.com/Remaker-Digital/groundtruth-kb`
- `E:\GT-KB\applications\Agent_Red\.git` remote: `https://github.com/mike-remakerdigital/agent-red`
- These are independent git repositories nested in the directory tree (not a monorepo, not git submodules).
- No other repositories may share the `E:\GT-KB\` directory tree without an owner-approved DELIB authorizing the addition.

ENFORCEMENT:
- This GOV will be registered as a cross-cutting requirement under GOV-CROSS-CUTTING-REQUIREMENTS-MECHANICAL-ENFORCEMENT-001 once the registry is populated; the registry's fast_check + deep_check for this rule are specified in companion DCL-AGENT-RED-NESTED-IN-APPLICATIONS-CHECK-001.
- Until the registry is live, voluntary compliance applies; Codex Loyal Opposition MUST cite this GOV in any review where a proposal touches files at `E:\GT-KB\` root that should belong under `applications\Agent_Red\`.

WAIVER POLICY:
- Temporary waivers (e.g., "this commit must touch a file at GT-KB root even though it's Agent-Red-related, because the file migration hasn't completed yet") are permitted ONLY via an owner-approved waiver DELIB carrying scope, expiry, and residual risk per the F3 schema (file-bridge-protocol.md Mandatory Specification Linkage Gate).
- The current state — Agent Red files at GT-KB root pending the migration project — is itself a known violation, tracked by the forthcoming GTKB-ISOLATION-018-AGENT-RED-FILE-MIGRATION bridge thread; until that completes, Agent-Red-related work at GT-KB root is the documented exception.

Rationale:
The Slice 8.6 incident (S330, 2026-05-04) demonstrated that the rules existed in CLAUDE.md and `.claude/rules/project-root-boundary.md` but were not visibly tied to a source-of-truth DELIB and were not mechanically enforced. This GOV closes that gap by formally tying the rules to DELIB-S330-AGENT-RED-NESTED-IN-APPLICATIONS-RULE (the retroactive owner-decision capture from S330) and making them subject to the cross-cutting-requirements mechanical enforcement layer.

SUPERSESSION:
This GOV supersedes the planning recommendations in DELIB-0879 to the extent they conflict. DELIB-0879's "Option B (separate sibling repos)" recommendation and "Option A as monorepo — Reject" verdict no longer apply; the owner's actual choice is the captured nested form specified by these 5 rules.

Owner approval: Approve as drafted (S330 chat, 2026-05-04, via AskUserQuestion).
explicit_change_request: Owner approved insertion via AskUserQuestion: "Approve as drafted". This artifact spawns from DELIB-S330-AGENT-RED-NESTED-IN-APPLICATIONS-RULE. Source intent: All GT-KB files must be in E:/GT-KB; all files in E:/GT-KB are GT-KB except files in E:/GT-KB/applications; all Agent Red files must be in E:/GT-KB/applications/Agent_Red; only deployed applications live in E:/GT-KB/applications, each in a named sub-directory.
change_reason: S330 spawn-queue item: GOV-AGENT-RED-NESTED-IN-APPLICATIONS-001 created from DELIB-S330-AGENT-RED-NESTED-IN-APPLICATIONS-RULE per just-approved DELIB-S330-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY transparency rule.
